| **_The Future Needs You_** You’ve seen the ads. You’ve been following the coverage. And through the [Right Track Results Tour](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b363dad94e&e=1acd9bb3d6) and other events, you’re seeing the candidates in communities across the state.

I think it's safe to say that we're officially nearing the peak of this year's critical midterm election. And what we do now will decide whether I'm writing to you in three weeks about victory or defeat.

After all, there are big questions in front of us now that will impact Hoosiers for generations.

Will we resoundingly defeat Democrat Joe Donnelly? Will we vote to keep our proven leaders **Connie Lawson, Tera Klutz** and **Kelly Mitchell** working for us at the Statehouse?

Or will it be a very different future? Will we have to live through another six years of Democrat Donnelly...as he joins his party boss Chuck Schumer and the resistance against President Trump? Will we let Democrats take the Statehouse back to the days of big spending, debt and dysfunction?

The good news is we have a say in what's next.

Will you [commit now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=49bfadd7a2&e=1acd9bb3d6) to at least an hour of making phone calls or knocking on doors between now and the election? It’s going to be a close race this year, and every voter we talk to counts.

Fill out [this form now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=329a7acf77&e=1acd9bb3d6), and our team will be in touch soon. Indiana's future needs you.

-Kyle Hupfer

_PHOTO: Chairman Kyle Hupfer rallied a sellout crowd of 1,000 Republicans on Friday night for the Indiana Republican Party Fall Dinner. We were excited to welcome our keynote speaker, Vice President Mike Pence, back to Indiana, along with our featured guest RNC Chairwoman Ronna McDaniel. _
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
|
---
---
| **_Honoring Our Veterans_**
This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** accompanied the final group of Lafayette-area veterans to visit our nation’s capital – and it was an emotional day.

As part of honor flights like this happening across the state and the nation, veterans visit the Washington, D.C. memorials honoring their service to our country as part of a busy, one-day trip. They then return home to a long-deserved hero’s welcome.

The trips started as a way to get surviving World War II vets to see these tributes, but have now expanded to allow other veterans – including Korea and Vietnam War veterans – to also have this experience.

**Governor Holcomb** visited the memorials on the National Mall and at Arlington National Cemetery with 84 Hoosier veterans and called it a “special day.” He said: “Those 84 Hoosier vets – the real deals – put life into perspective. ... They had our backs, I’ll spend the rest of my life having theirs.”

Check out more photos from Governor Holcomb's trip with these veterans [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7b03cb9687&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
|
---
| | **_The Mike Braun Corner_**
---
|
---
|
---
---
| **_Endorsements, Hog Roasts & TV Ads!_**
Look around – from your TV to your community – and you’re likely to run into our hardworking U.S. Senate candidate, **Mike Braun**!

Last weekend, he stood beside our very own **Vice President Mike Pence** in Indianapolis for the kickoff of the Right Track Results tour, joined Republicans in Knox County for their annual spot shoot, and attended a tour stop in his hometown of Jasper – the place where it all started.

This week, **Mike Braun** was endorsed by the Indiana Manufacturers Association, and said, “Hoosiers deserve someone who sounds the same on the factory floor as he does on the Senate floor.” He also visited White County, where he attended their local hog roast.

And there’s certainly no shortage of **Mike Braun** sightings on Hoosier airwaves! This week the campaign released a new ad, called Always. It’s a powerful ad and tells the story of **Mike Braun’s** commitment to providing quality, affordable healthcare to his employees, because nobody should go broke because they get sick. [Watch it here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e6e3dd4982&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

This weekend, **Mike Braun** has more stops planned on the [Right Track Results Tour](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e488e87565&e=1acd9bb3d6), coming soon to a community near you!

---
| **_Rally With Donald Trump Jr.!_**
This Monday, **Donald Trump, Jr.** – the eldest son of**President Donald Trump** – is coming to Indiana!

Join him at the Indianapolis Regional Airport in Greenfield for an EPIC event, where we’ll be rallying in support of our Right Track Results Team – **Mike Braun, Greg Pence, Connie Lawson, Tera Klutz** and **Kelly Mitchell**!

He’ll be talking about why he and his dad are HUGE fans of **Mike Braun** , and why it’s critical that he’s in the U.S. Senate as a partner for **President Trump**. It’s an event you’re not going to want to miss, so [RVSP today](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=287e6b87d2&e=1acd9bb3d6) and make sure you’re there!

| **_Follow the Right Track Results Tour!_**

Your team of proven Republican leaders are crisscrossing Indiana between now and Election Day, visiting all corners of the state to talk about the RESULTS they’re delivering for Hoosiers!

>From early vote rallies, meet and greets and door-to-door kickoffs, we’ll be there. Tomorrow, we’ll be in Indianapolis, Corydon, Columbus and Greensburg.

Visit [RightTrackResults.com](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=91237ce99f&e=1acd9bb3d6) to see when we’ll be in your area, and follow along with the tour with the hashtag #RightTrackResults!

| **_A Decade of Tax Cuts!_**
Under more than a decade of Republican leadership at the Statehouse, your taxes are now lower – allowing you to keep more of your hard earned money!

Along with eliminating the death tax and inventory taxes, we've also capped property taxes and cut corporate and individual income tax rates! Because of our strong statewide leadership, Indiana's taxes are going down!

And through the Right Track Results Tour and other events, you’re seeing the candidates... | | | | | | | | | The Future Needs You You’ve seen the ads. You’ve been following the coverage. And through the and other events, you’re seeing the candidates in communities across the state. I think it's safe to say that we're officially nearing the peak of this year's critical midterm election. And what we do now will decide whether I'm writing to you in three weeks about victory or defeat. After all, there are big questions in front of us now that will impact Hoosiers for generations. Will we resoundingly defeat Democrat Joe Donnelly? Will we vote to keep our proven leaders Connie Lawson, Tera Klutz and Kelly Mitchell working for us at the Statehouse? Or will it be a very different future? Will we have to live through another six years of Democrat Donnelly...as he joins his party boss Chuck Schumer and the resistance against President Trump? Will we let Democrats take the Statehouse back to the days of big spending, debt and dysfunction? The good news is we have a say in what's next. Will you to at least an hour of making phone calls or knocking on doors between now and the election? It’s going to be a close race this year, and every voter we talk to counts. Fill out , and our team will be in touch soon. Indiana's future needs you. -Kyle Hupfer PHOTO: Chairman Kyle Hupfer rallied a sellout crowd of 1,000 Republicans on Friday night for the Indiana Republican Party Fall Dinner. We were excited to welcome our keynote speaker, Vice President Mike Pence, back to Indiana, along with our featured guest RNC Chairwoman Ronna McDaniel. | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | | Honoring Our Veterans This week, Governor Eric Holcomb accompanied the final group of Lafayette-area veterans to visit our nation’s capital – and it was an emotional day. As part of honor flights like this happening across the state and the nation, veterans visit the Washington, D.C. memorials honoring their service to our country as part of a busy, one-day trip. They then return home to a long-deserved hero’s welcome. The trips started as a way to get surviving World War II vets to see these tributes, but have now expanded to allow other veterans – including Korea and Vietnam War veterans – to also have this experience. Governor Holcomb visited the memorials on the National Mall and at Arlington National Cemetery with 84 Hoosier veterans and called it a “special day.” He said: “Those 84 Hoosier vets – the real deals – put life into perspective. ... They had our backs, I’ll spend the rest of my life having theirs.” Check out more photos from Governor Holcomb's trip with these veterans ! | | | The Mike Braun Corner | | | Endorsements, Hog Roasts & TV Ads! Look around – from your TV to your community – and you’re likely to run into our hardworking U.S. Senate candidate, Mike Braun! Last weekend, he stood beside our very own Vice President Mike Pence in Indianapolis for the kickoff of the Right Track Results tour, joined Republicans in Knox County for their annual spot shoot, and attended a tour stop in his hometown of Jasper – the place where it all started. This week, Mike Braun was endorsed by the Indiana Manufacturers Association, and said, “Hoosiers deserve someone who sounds the same on the factory floor as he does on the Senate floor.” He also visited White County, where he attended their local hog roast. And there’s certainly no shortage of Mike Braun sightings on Hoosier airwaves! This week the campaign released a new ad, called Always. It’s a powerful ad and tells the story of Mike Braun’s commitment to providing quality, affordable healthcare to his employees, because nobody should go broke because they get sick. ! This weekend, Mike Braun has more stops planned on the , coming soon to a community near you! | | | Rally With Donald Trump Jr.! This Monday, Donald Trump, Jr. – the eldest son ofPresident Donald Trump – is coming to Indiana! Join him at the Indianapolis Regional Airport in Greenfield for an EPIC event, where we’ll be rallying in support of our Right Track Results Team – Mike Braun, Greg Pence, Connie Lawson, Tera Klutz and Kelly Mitchell! He’ll be talking about why he and his dad are HUGE fans of Mike Braun , and why it’s critical that he’s in the U.S. Senate as a partner for President Trump. It’s an event you’re not going to want to miss, so and make sure you’re there! | | | Follow the Right Track Results Tour! is ON! Your team of proven Republican leaders are crisscrossing Indiana between now and Election Day, visiting all corners of the state to talk about the RESULTS they’re delivering for Hoosiers! From early vote rallies, meet and greets and door-to-door kickoffs, we’ll be there. Tomorrow, we’ll be in Indianapolis, Corydon, Columbus and Greensburg. Visit to see when we’ll be in your area, and follow along with the tour with the hashtag #RightTrackResults! | | | Early Voting Is Now Open! body:
Activist - Early voting has begun in [many states ](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/6/DB/r5cs/6fx4eG/vXrkQyCh2L/P/P)for the most important election in our lifetime. So let's urge our Democratic friends in those states to vote early and then convince their Democratic friends to vote early too. 1\. **[Download the VoteWithMe app](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/s/pZ/r5cs/6fx4eG/z70L4rTAKS/P/P) **(for iPhone and Android)**** to see which of your Democratic and progressive friends and relatives are registered in an Early Voting state.**[Urge them to vote today!](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/v/pZ/r5cs/6fx4eG/s89EXFwPrG/P/P)** [](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/z/pZ/r5cs/6fx4eG/zgG7kUv7rH/P/P) 2\. **[Join The Last Weekend's army of volunteers](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/U/DN/r5cs/6fx4eG/6H6594ehCa/P/P) **to visit your neighbors the weekend before Election Day (Tuesday, November 6) and [**convince them to vote for Democrats**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/j/DN/r5cs/6fx4eG/zl087UpQ18/P/P). 3\. [**Buy our beautiful Blue Wave Voters T-Shirts and caps**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/5/Dt/r5cs/6fx4eG/zVbyRMeLSX/P/P)to show your pride while you knock doors or just go shopping. They are 100% made in the USA and union printed. They also make great gifts for your passionate Democratic friends! [](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/r/DG/r5cs/6fx4eG/vzn6bRX394/P/P) 4\. [**Join the Blue Wave Voters Facebook group**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/Y/pz/r5cs/6fx4eG/6qeA1mOwR3/P/P)to connect with other activists and help us build the Blue Wave. Our Blue Wave is strong - **[Democrats have an 8% advantage in the polls](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/e/Dp/r5cs/6fx4eG/U3asXYPetF/P/P)**. But Trump is firing up his angry supporters to vote, so we must persuade every single Democrat to vote too. Let's build the biggest Blue Wave in history! Bob Fertik p.s. Learn more about early voting in your state [here](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/N7yVD/3/DD/r5cs/6fx4eG/sjJDve31Zs/P/P).

Jeff Cox reports that job openings hit a record 7.136 million for the month of August, according to the Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey. “Openings dwarfed the total level of workers looking for jobs, which stood at 6.23 million for that month and fell to 5.96 million in September, recent Labor Department statistics show.” **[Trump Smashes Regs, Cuts $33 Billion vs. $245 Billion Added by Obama](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgxMDIwLjk2NTIwNjMxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MTAyMC45NjUyMDYzMSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4NDAzNjYyJmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&102&&&https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/washington-secrets/trump-smashes-regs-cuts-33-billion-vs-245-billion-added-by-obama)** _-Washington Examiner_
“President Trump’s promise to cut Obama-era regulations has gone into overdrive, with the slashing generating $33 billion in savings, far more than expected,” Paul Bedard reports. “A new report from the White House budget office said that $23 billion in savings has occurred this year alone.” **[The Trump Administration’s Deregulation Efforts are Saving Billions of Dollars](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgxMDIwLjk2NTIwNjMxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MTAyMC45NjUyMDYzMSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4NDAzNjYyJmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&103&&&https://www.washingtonpost.com/opinions/the-trump-administration-is-deregulating-at-breakneck-speed/2018/10/17/09bd0b4c-d194-11e8-83d6-291fcead2ab1_story.html?utm_term=.45f2df7ff17b)** _-The Washington Post_
In _The Washington Post_ , Neomi Rao, Administrator of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs, explains how the Trump Administration has cut regulations at breakneck speed. “When reviewing regulations, we start with a simple question: What is the problem this regulation is trying to fix? Unless otherwise required by law, we move forward only when we can identify a serious problem or market failure that would be best addressed by federal regulation.” **[Jobless Claims Drop 5,000 to 210,000 in Mid-October, Stick Near 50-Year Low](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgxMDIwLjk2NTIwNjMxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MTAyMC45NjUyMDYzMSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4NDAzNjYyJmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&104&&&https://www.marketwatch.com/story/jobless-claims-drop-5000-to-210000-in-mid-october-2018-10-18)** _-MarketWatch_
“It can’t get a whole lot better in the U.S. job market. Openings just hit a record high, the U.S. unemployment rate has fallen to a 48-year low of 3.7% and hiring remains robust,” Jeffry Bartash reports. The strong demand for American labor is pushing up wages and benefits, as well, fueling the longest economic expansion in U.S. history. **[Signs of Stability in Individual Health Care Market](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgxMDIwLjk2NTIwNjMxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MTAyMC45NjUyMDYzMSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4NDAzNjYyJmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&105&&&https://www.whitehouse.gov/articles/signs-stability-individual-health-care-market/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=rr)** -Fox News
“As a result of ObamaCare, average premiums on the federal exchange jumped by 105 percent between 2013 and 2017,” CMS Administrator Seema Verma writes. But with President Trump’s reforms, “fortunately, these chaotic trends are now turning. For the first time since ObamaCare was enacted, the premium for a typical federal exchange plan—while still much too high—will drop.” **[Trump Visits Hurricane-Torn Florida, Georgia](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgxMDIwLjk2NTIwNjMxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MTAyMC45NjUyMDYzMSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4NDAzNjYyJmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&106&&&https://www.wsj.com/articles/trump-visits-hurricane-torn-florida-offers-praise-and-support-1539627865)** -_The Wall Street Journal_
President Trump, along with the First Lady, “toured hurricane-torn areas of Florida and Georgia on Monday, declaring that ‘nobody has seen anything like this’ as crews continued to restore power and residents grappled with the devastation,” Alex Leary reports. Addressing the work of first responders and volunteers, the President said the job they’ve done “has been incredible.”
**[Finally, the Government Has Taken Steps to Stop Releasing Unaccompanied Minors to Criminals and Traffickers](http://links.govdelivery.com:80/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbXNpZD0mYXVpZD0mbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgxMDIwLjk2NTIwNjMxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MTAyMC45NjUyMDYzMSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE4NDAzNjYyJmVtYWlsaWQ9ZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20mdXNlcmlkPWRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tJnRhcmdldGlkPSZmbD0mZXh0cmE9TXVsdGl2YXJpYXRlSWQ9JiYm&&&107&&&https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/opinion/op-eds/finally-the-government-has-taken-steps-to-stop-releasing-unaccompanied-minors-to-criminals-and-traffickers)** -_Washington Examiner_ “The Obama administration released thousands of unaccompanied alien children to criminal sponsors,” writes Dr. Jason Piccolo, a former ICE Supervisor. “I know because I was the Homeland Security whistleblower who legally came forward in late 2015 to the U.S. Office of Special Counsel and to Sen. Chuck Grassley’s office about the releases . . . Only now is this serious and dangerous problem finally being dealt with.”

body:
[](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e7c9c/51dbcc2a/17277a1e/3ad4368/733211542/VEsH/ "https://bobcasey.com/") Here's a preview into one of the first decisions that Pennsylvania's next senator will need to make:

Mitch McConnell and the Trump administration have made it clear that, after this election, **they're putting massive cuts to programs like Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security on the table**. They need to pay for the corporate tax cut they passed last year, and they want to send the bill directly to working families and seniors.

If I'm re-elected, I'll fight that plan with everything I've got. It's despicable. It's cruel. Pennsylvanians deserve better. But if I lose, I don't trust Lou Barletta to take up that fight. He voted for these tax cuts, knowing they would have to be paid for eventually. He's supported plans to end Medicare as we know it. He votes with the President 99% of the time. He looks out for far-right special interests, not Pennsylvania families.

**I need your help to win re-election so that I can fight this dangerous agenda, which would hurt so many people here at home. Congressman Barletta caught up to me in fundraising last quarter, and if we don't act fast, he might outraise us before Election Day. There are only 17 days left in this campaign, so we can't fall behind. Please, if you can, make a donation to support my campaign.**

Here's the best link: **_[secure.bobcasey.com/donate](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20181020_fr1_nd_pa&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Thanks, as always, for your support,

Bob

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Climate crisis -- here's my plan of action:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
I'm prepared to take drastic action to fight climate change. Are you with me?

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7bb3c/2f5ea88d/744facb5/2cbd9d64/2644516550/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/climate-leaders-now?utm_source=ema_2018-10-20_mid-month-2-resend&refcode=ema_2018-10-20_mid-month-2-resend&utm_campaign=mid-month-2-resend&utm_medium=ema") Friend, From rising sea levels, to devastating droughts, to torrential storms, the IPCC report out of the United Nations makes it brutally clear that warming will make the world worse for us in the forms of famine, disease, economic tolls, and refugee crises. **But enough talk about disasters. What can we do about it? Here's the plan:**
* We must transform our economy: We'll reduce carbon pollution in our power and transportation sectors with new energy solutions and green infrastructure.
* We'll need to invest in developing and deploying clean energy technologies for the 21st century and create tens of thousands of jobs.
* We'll need to eliminate the unabated carbon pollution from coal-fired power plants.
* And we'll need our political leaders to take extraordinary action, starting right now.

[**It can all be done, friend, but we need to demand action from our political leaders. Will you chip in to help me hold them accountable to fighting climate change before it's too late?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7bb3c/2f5ea88d/744facb5/2cbd9d7b/2644516550/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/climate-leaders-now?utm_source=ema_2018-10-20_mid-month-2-resend&refcode=ema_2018-10-20_mid-month-2-resend&utm_campaign=mid-month-2-resend&utm_medium=emaa") There's no beating around the bush: Keeping warming to levels that limit global climate disasters will be very, very hard. But we have the science, the technology, and the financial means to make it happen. All we lack is the national political leadership to mobilize the unprecedented steps we must take to stabilize temperature rise, right now. **We can do it, but we need political leadership -- leaders who actually give a damn. So that's why I'm asking for your help; will you join me in the fight to power our climate movement right now?** | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7bb3c/2f5ea88d/744facb5/2cbd9d64/2644516550/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/climate-leaders-now?utm_source=ema_2018-10-20_mid-month-2-resend&refcode=ema_2018-10-20_mid-month-2-resend&utm_campaign=mid-month-2-resend&utm_medium=ema")
---
Thanks for your help. Together, we can take the action we need to reduce our carbon emissions before 2030 and save our world from the worst impacts of climate change.
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: I don't expect anyone to vote for me because I am a Black woman.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
Representation matters. Black women have played critical, and usually thankless, roles in our nation since its founding. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa73f/34a064cc/130afb5/70d7afc/2898712940/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friend - As I outline my plans to lead Georgia forward, I am often asked about the historic nature of my campaign - the possibility of once again being "the first." The fact is that if Georgia voters put their faith in me, I will be the first Black woman governor in U.S. history. **It's who I am, but it's not why I am running.** Those who've worked with me know that I believe in digging into policy and I care deeply about the details. I ran for office in 2006 because I wanted to use policy to improve lives. It's why my website has more than 100 single-spaced pages outlining my comprehensive plans for veterans, for economic mobility, for affordable child care, criminal justice reform, higher education, gun safety, the environment and much more. As Democratic Leader in the Georgia House, I fought for our communities with facts and figures and by reading the fine print. Actually, I once used a color-coded spreadsheet to stop one of the largest tax increases in Georgia history. **Using policy to get good done isn't just a part of who I am - it's why I am running for governor.** [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa73f/34a064cc/130afb5/70d7afd/2898712940/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMDIwMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-october?refcode=IE102018-full") **But at the same time, representation matters.** Black women have played critical, and usually thankless, roles in our nation since its founding. But a look at who leads our cities, our states, and our nation tells a much different story. I've been "the first" before, and in November, with your help, I'll be the first again - and able to use the office of governor to effect real, lasting change. But here's the thing: What's even more exciting than being "the first" is knowing that I won't be the last. I want my nieces to look at their Aunt Stacey and say, "She's in charge of the state of Georgia!" More importantly, I want them to see that they could be, too. If we can see it, we can be it. I don't expect anyone to vote for me because I am Black. I don't expect anyone to vote for me because I am a woman. But I expect everyone to know that as a Black woman, there are experiences that I've had - and barriers that I've overcome - that show my resilience, my fortitude, and my ability to be a strong leader for every Georgia family. **[If you're with me and ready to build a Georgia where everyone is represented, and where everyone has the freedom and opportunity to thrive, please contribute $3 to support our people-powered campaign. Every dollar helps us reach out to voters and lead Georgia forward.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa73f/34a064cc/130afb5/70d7afd/2898712940/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMDIwMTgtZnVsbCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-october?refcode=IE102018-full")** Thank you, as always, for standing with me. clean_body: Representation matters. Black women have played critical, and usually thankless, roles in our nation since its founding. | "" | | | Friend - As I outline my plans to lead Georgia forward, I am often asked about the historic nature of my campaign - the possibility of once again being "the first." The fact is that if Georgia voters put their faith in me, I will be the first Black woman governor in U.S. history. It's who I am, but it's not why I am running. Those who've worked with me know that I believe in digging into policy and I care deeply about the details. I ran for office in 2006 because I wanted to use policy to improve lives. It's why my website has more than 100 single-spaced pages outlining my comprehensive plans for veterans, for economic mobility, for affordable child care, criminal justice reform, higher education, gun safety, the environment and much more. As Democratic Leader in the Georgia House, I fought for our communities with facts and figures and by reading the fine print. Actually, I once used a color-coded spreadsheet to stop one of the largest tax increases in Georgia history. Using policy to get good done isn't just a part of who I am - it's why I am running for governor. "" But at the same time, representation matters. Black women have played critical, and usually thankless, roles in our nation since its founding. But a look at who leads our cities, our states, and our nation tells a much different story. I've been "the first" before, and in November, with your help, I'll be the first again - and able to use the office of governor to effect real, lasting change. But here's the thing: What's even more exciting than being "the first" is knowing that I won't be the last. I want my nieces to look at their Aunt Stacey and say, "She's in charge of the state of Georgia!" More importantly, I want them to see that they could be, too. If we can see it, we can be it. I don't expect anyone to vote for me because I am Black. I don't expect anyone to vote for me because I am a woman. But I expect everyone to know that as a Black woman, there are experiences that I've had - and barriers that I've overcome - that show my resilience, my fortitude, and my ability to be a strong leader for every Georgia family. "" Thank you, as always, for standing with me. Stacey Abrams "" | "" "" "" 1270 Caroline Street Suite D120-447 Atlanta, GA 30307 (C)2018 Stacey Abrams for Governor, All rights reserved "" | | Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Day 1: Huge crowds in Indiana and Michigan
body:
[1]Bernie Sanders

Sisters and Brothers -

We started our eight-day, nine-state tour yesterday with stops in
Bloomington, Indiana to campaign for Liz Watson, a strong progressive
running for Congress there, and in Ann Arbor, Michigan to campaign for
another progressive running for governor, Gretchen Whitmer.

And in just a moment, I am going to ask you to [ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1001&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ]make a $3
donation to both of their campaigns. This is important.

Because if you are concerned about the disastrous Trump policies, if you
are concerned about one-party rule in the House, the Senate and in too
many states across the country, then it is important we do all we can to
support progressives running for office in these final weeks.

First, let me talk about our rally in Indiana. The turnout was tremendous:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1002&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ][IMG]

Liz Watson is exciting people all throughout her district in Indiana
because she is running on a platform of Medicare for all, free tuition at
public colleges and universities, and a $15 minimum wage.

She is building the kind of grassroots campaign that scares Donald Trump
and his friends.

It scares them because they want us to sit out in this election. They
don’t want you to vote.

The truth is in 2014 they had their way. We had the lowest voter turnout
for a midterm election at any time since World War II and Republicans won
landslide victories.

This year, that has got to change – big time. This year, instead of having
the lowest voter turnout in modern history, we need to have the highest
midterm voter turnout ever.

We need high turnout by people everywhere who are tired of working longer
hours for lower wages.

We need high turnout everywhere by people who cannot afford health
insurance, or by young people who can’t afford to go to college or leave
school with massive amounts of debt.

We need high turnout by people everywhere who are concerned about women’s
ability to control their own bodies.

We need high turnout everywhere by people who are disgusted by an
administration that foments racism, xenophobia, sexism, religious bigotry
and homophobia.

We need high turnout everywhere by people who are sick and tired of having
a president who is a pathological liar and a Congress that caves to his
every whim.

That is why as soon as our rally was complete, we led hundreds of people
down the street to vote in Liz’s election. It was an extraordinary scene:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1003&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ][IMG]

Liz is in a close race. It’s a race she can win. And if you are with her,
and if you are with Gretchen Whitmer, give them the financial resources to
win on election night. That is why I have to ask:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1028&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ]Can you split a $3 donation between Liz Watson’s
campaign for Congress, Gretchen Whitmer’s campaign for governor, and my
work organizing and rallying progressives across the country ahead of the
November elections? This is important.

The next stop on our tour was in Michigan, where I attended a rally at the
University of Michigan in Ann Arbor with Democratic candidate for
governor, Gretchen Whitmer.

The enthusiasm for Gretchen’s campaign and for our visit was tremendous.
Take a look at these photo from outside the event before it even began:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1029&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ][IMG]

And what we talked about at that event was the critical need for Michigan
to clean up its drinking water and invest in infrastructure.

We talked about the importance of a $15 minimum wage that would lift up
working people in communities all across the state.

And we talked about ideas like universal preschool and the legalization of
marijuana.

It was another great meeting. The auditorium was filled to capacity:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1030&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ][IMG]

So were two overflow rooms.

And what I want to tell you about that first day of meetings and rallies
is something that will not come as a surprise to many of you.

And that is, if there is low voter turnout, right-wing Republicans are
going to control the House and the Senate.

But if turnout is large, if young people and working people across this
country are as motivated to vote as were those who were at our meetings
yesterday, then Democrats will gain control of the House and the Senate.

And if we do that, we can change the country.

But I am here to tell you that we cannot sit back and think someone else
will do this for us.

We have to do it ourselves.

And while that starts with voting, it does not end there. It also means
doing all we can to support candidates like Liz Watson and Gretchen
Whitmer between now and Election Day. That is why I am asking:

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1055&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ]Can you split a $3 donation between Liz Watson’s
campaign for Congress, Gretchen Whitmer’s campaign for governor, and my
work organizing and rallying progressives across the country ahead of the
November elections? This is important.

Today I am in South Carolina, one of the poorest states in this country,
which currently suffers under right-wing, Republican control. Later in the
day, I’ll be off to Iowa – then it’s Wisconsin, Arizona, Colorado, Nevada
and California after that. I will keep you up-to-date as the tour
progresses. If we are visiting a state you are in, I hope to see you
there. Otherwise, I will keep you up to date on email and social media.

In solidarity,

Bernie Sanders

[ https://act.berniesanders.com/go/5429?refcode=em181020-full&t=1056&akid=245%2E150750%2EFs3xGm ]DONATE

name: Dana Bye
subject: Every day is Election Day in VA-7!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Vote by mail is already underway here in Virginia -- which means voters are already casting their ballots, and every day is Election Day from now until November 6th!
| | Peter, We're counting down the days until Election Day here in VA-7, as we often remind you _(the latest count: 17 days to go until we flip this district!)._ But that countdown is not the whole picture. After all, vote by mail and absentee voting are already underway here in Virginia -- which means voters are already casting their ballots, **and every day is Election Day from now until November 6th!** That's why Team Spanberger is implementing an ambitious field effort specifically for the early vote. With the support of our tremendous team of volunteers, now 4,000+ strong, our field staffers are working hard to help our voters request ballots, if they would like to vote by mail. We are also working to identify which voters are most likely to turn their ballots in prior to November 6th, and ensuring that we reach them before they cast their votes. And, of course, all these efforts require resources -- so we are counting on this grassroots team to keep it up. subject: Some thoughts on the debate yesterday...
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY IAN TODD FOR CONGRESS
body:
** We were underestimated.
------------------------------------------------------------
Yesterday was a thrill, listening to Ian debate Tom Emmer live on MPR was easily one of the more stressful moments of the campaign for me. I know that Ian came prepared with the facts and a positive attitude, but you never really can know what your opponent will say before he says it. Despite the initial stress I quickly became energized and excited to hear Ian artfully outline why it is critical that we move towards a single-payer health care system and why the status quo is not good enough for the people of Minnesota.

I was also a bit baffled by some of the points that Tom Emmer tried to make. He made the dubious claim that Medicare for all is actually "Medicare for none". He also claimed that people are happy with massive tax cuts and denied that Mitch McConnel has said anything about cutting Social Security (he has just this past week). It seems clear that Tom Emmer came unprepared for direct, informed, and robust answers to his claims. Ian walked away from that debate a proven contender ready to stand up for the people of this district.

I will send out a link to the debate as soon as it is made publicly available. Until then, I think the key takeaway from this debate is that Tom Emmer is comfortable resting on his laurels despite that fact that his very own district is struggling to afford healthcare. That is why I am asking you to donate today (https://secure.actblue.com/donate/iantodd?refcode=postdebateMPR&amount=100&utm_source=Newsletter&utm_campaign=2dc751785c-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_10_20_09_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_28710cd7a6-2dc751785c-9291445) so that we can fight for change and make sure that our leadership is fighting for everyone, and not just their wealthy donors.
